metadata.summary: Convention for writing AI-readable directory context files: CLAUDE.md (single line: @AGENTS.md) and AGENTS.md (20-80 lines covering purpose, file table, key types, commands, gotchas). Required in every directory with source code — not just repo roots but also subpackages, module folders, test dirs.

content.title: CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md Documentation
content.summary: Convention for writing AI-readable directory context files: CLAUDE.md (single line: @AGENTS.md) and AGENTS.md (20-80 lines covering purpose, file table, key types, commands, gotchas). Required in every directory with source code — not just repo roots but also subpackages, module folders, test dirs.

why.p: Without per-directory context, agents load the whole repo tree or hallucinate structure. The CLAUDE.md = @AGENTS.md pattern bridges Claude Code with other tools (Cursor, Cline, Codex). Capping AGENTS.md at 80 lines enforces routing-first: agents narrow the search per level, never bulk-load. The "Gotchas" section is the highest-value content — it corrects training-data assumptions unique to this specific codebase.

list.item: Creating a new directory with code: add CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md before any other file.
Onboarding an agent to an unfamiliar module — agent reads AGENTS.md to route, then loads specific files.
After a significant refactor: regenerate AGENTS.md so future context loads stay accurate.
Multi-agent workspaces where each tool reads a different filename.
Repos with many sub-packages where loading the whole tree blows context budget.
Tiny one-file scripts — a README is sufficient.
Generated / vendored code (node_modules/, dist/) — never document these.
Highly volatile prototypes where structure changes daily — document after dust settles.
Directories that are pure data (assets, fixtures) without logic.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md must contain exactly one line: @AGENTS.md. Nothing else. Any content added to CLAUDE.md belongs in AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md is an entrypoint, not a doc. The @AGENTS.md import directive tells Claude Code to auto-load AGENTS.md. Putting content here bypasses the convention and creates a parallel source of truth.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md target length: 20-80 lines. Hard cap: 120 lines. If it exceeds 80, split detailed content into .agents/ reference docs and link from AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: Routing-first principle: agents narrow search per directory level. A 500-line AGENTS.md negates load-on-demand and wastes context budget on every load.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md must include: Summary (1 sentence), file table (name → purpose), key types/exports, common commands (exact as in package.json/Makefile), and Gotchas.
The Gotchas section is mandatory and is the highest-value content. Extract from: TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK comments in source and recent fix commit messages.
rule.rationale: Gotchas correct training-data assumptions unique to this codebase. Without them, agents reproduce the same bugs documented in fix commits.

rule.statement: CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md are required in every directory that contains source code — not just repo roots, but also subpackages, module folders, test dirs.
Skip only: empty __init__.py-only dirs with no logic, generated/vendored dirs (node_modules/, dist/), and pure-data dirs (assets/, fixtures/).
Reference commands exactly as they exist in package.json or Makefile. Verify with grep before writing. Never invent commands that look plausible.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated commands are the most common silent breakage in AGENTS.md. They look correct and agents execute them, failing silently.

rule.statement: Backend AGENTS.md must include: Models table (model → purpose), Services table (function → purpose), API Endpoints table (method/path → purpose), key config vars.

example.note: Backend AGENTS.md extras: models table, services, endpoints, and where to find Django settings/celery/urls.

rule.statement: Frontend AGENTS.md must include: Components table, state management location and pattern, routing table (path → component), and build commands.
Infra AGENTS.md must include: Resources table (file → what it creates), Environments table (env → description), key commands (apply, plan, destroy), and secrets management note.
Library AGENTS.md must include: Public API table (function/class → purpose), usage example, version constraints, and breaking-change history if any.

section.name: universal-required-sections

list.item: Gotchas — from comments and fix commits

section.reference: Universal AGENTS.md template with all required sections

antipattern.description: Writing 500-line AGENTS.md with full API reference, changelog, and architecture diagrams inline.
antipattern.reason: A 500-line AGENTS.md is loaded in full every time an agent enters the directory. Bulk context load negates the routing-first principle. Detail belongs in .agents/ reference docs.

antipattern.description: Writing npm test or pytest in Common Commands without verifying the commands exist in package.json or Makefile.
antipattern.reason: Agents execute commands from AGENTS.md verbatim. A hallucinated command fails silently or produces a misleading error that wastes investigation time.

antipattern.description: AGENTS.md with no Gotchas section because "there are no obvious gotchas."
antipattern.reason: Gotchas are always present; they are just not obvious from reading the directory. They live in TODO/FIXME comments and fix commit messages. Skipping the section guarantees agents reproduce known bugs.

antipattern.description: Source files updated but AGENTS.md not regenerated, causing stale file tables and outdated commands.
antipattern.reason: Agents route based on AGENTS.md. Stale tables send agents to deleted files or miss newly created entry points.

rule.statement: Run the audit-agents-md.sh drift detector before each release to flag directories where AGENTS.md is older than source files by more than 14 days.

antipattern.description: Including API keys, environment variable values, or references to .env file paths with secrets in AGENTS.md.
antipattern.reason: AGENTS.md is committed to the repo. Secrets leak via grep and log forwarding. Reference env var names only, never values.

template.purpose: Universal AGENTS.md template with all required sections.
